Update github.com/vmware/govmomi to v0.51.0 (#44566)

Update github.com/vmware/govmomi to v0.51.0 which includes a fix to the
test simulator so it no longer panics when GODEBUG=fips140=only is set.
Remove the special test filtering used in metricbeat that was used to
avoid this and replace with the standard goFIPSOnlyUnitTest target.

Run filebeat and metricbeat go integration tests with fips140=only (#44441)

Run filebeat and metricebeat Go Integration tests with GODEBUG=fips140=only set.

x-pack/filebeat/input/cel: fix handling of ADC metadata server credentials (#44571)

CEL input do not handle ADC metadata server credentials correctly.
Currently they expect a JSON key in ADC (Application Default
Credentials) which is not present when Filebeat is running on Google
Cloud Platform, as described in Google's SDK[1].

[1] https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/oauth2/google#Credentials

[Chore] Replace global logger instances with local logger #6 (#44489)

* [Chore] Replace global logger instances with local logger
doc updates

[AutoOps ES] Add New `autoops_es` module to X-Pack Metricbeat (#44565)

This adds the AutoOps ES Metricbeat module to the X-Pack codebase.

The expectation for this module is for it to be used internally and
externally with a documented configuration to enable end users to
ship AutoOps metricset data to the AutoOps Service for processing.

While effectively allowed, it is currently expected that data is
going to be shipped directly to Elasticsearch without some data
enrichment.

filebeat/input/v2: avoid race condition in elastic-agent-libs/monitoring (#44303)

This change fixes the v2 input to avoid a race condition in
elastic-agent-libs/monitoring that could be triggered when multiple goroutines
try to create pipeline clients simultaneously, such as in the AWS S3/SQS input
which creates multiple worker goroutines, each with its own client.

The root cause of the issue is https://github.com/elastic/elastic-agent-libs/issues/319.
The function creating new monitoring variables should be thread-safe, but there
is a bug in their synchronisation, which the v2 input was hitting.

The fix introduces v2.PrepareInputMetrics, which abstracts away wrapping the
beat.PipelineConnector to add a beat.ClientListener to collect metrics per-input
in a way to avoid creating the monitoring variables when `ConnectWith` is called.
addressed Dan's suggestions
